text.title: Solution Assessment — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Solution Assessment must enforce.

rule.statement: Run all four assessment types at distinct points: Design (pre-build), Implementation (mid-build), Deployment (go-live), Post-Implementation (30/60/90 days).
rule.rationale: A single end-of-project assessment misses fixable implementation errors and lacks a value baseline.

rule.statement: Every metric verdict MUST cite at least one evidence URL with retrieved_at timestamp; no verdicts from memory, screenshots, or verbal reports.
rule.rationale: Without primary-source citation, agents hallucinate KPI values and humans report the number that supports their preferred conclusion.

rule.statement: Every metric row includes sample size; a metric hit on n=12 is statistically inconclusive and may not be called 'Met'.
rule.rationale: Small samples produce variance indistinguishable from noise; underpowered 'success' verdicts waste follow-on investment.

rule.statement: Triangulate every business-value metric with at least two independent sources (analytics + finance, or analytics + survey).
rule.rationale: Single-source verdicts are gameable; two independent sources must agree before a verdict is reliable.

rule.statement: Mark requirements as 'Deprecated' (not 'Not Met') when the underlying business need has changed since the requirement was written.
rule.rationale: Forcing Met/Not Met on stale requirements produces misleading reports and penalises delivery teams unfairly.
